query: traditional Milanese osso buco with saffron risotto for a home kitchen

draft.title: Osso Buco with Saffron Risotto
draft.description: Traditional Milanese osso buco paired with creamy saffron risotto, perfect for a comforting home-cooked meal.

ingredients.name: veal shank
ingredients.note: cut into 4 pieces, about 3-4 cm thick

ingredients.name: dry white wine
ingredients.note: 250 ml for osso buco, 150 ml for risotto

steps.text: Pat veal shanks dry and dredge lightly in flour, shaking off excess.
Heat olive oil and half the butter in a large heavy pan over medium-high heat; brown veal shanks on all sides, then remove and set aside.
Add chopped onion, carrot, celery, and garlic to the pan; sauté until softened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in tomato paste and cook for 1 minute, then deglaze with 250 ml white wine, scraping up browned bits.
Return veal to the pan; add warm beef stock to cover halfway. Bring to a simmer, cover, and cook gently for 1.5 to 2 hours until meat is tender.
Meanwhile, soak saffron threads in 2 tbsp warm water.
For risotto, melt remaining butter in a saucepan, add shallot and cook until translucent.
Add arborio rice and stir to coat grains with butter; toast for 2 minutes.
Pour in 150 ml white wine and cook until mostly absorbed.
Add warm beef stock gradually, one ladle at a time, stirring constantly and allowing liquid to absorb before adding more.
Halfway through cooking, add saffron with its soaking water to the risotto.
When rice is al dente and creamy (about 18-20 minutes), stir in grated parmesan, season with salt and pepper, and remove from heat.
Mix lemon zest and parsley to make gremolata.
Serve veal shanks over saffron risotto, spoon pan sauce on top, and sprinkle with gremolata.
